questions.q: In which year was the Mandal Commission set up?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission was set up in 1979.

questions.q: In which year did the Mandal Commission submit its report?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission submitted its report in 1980.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission examined the conditions of which groups?
questions.explanation: The Commission examined the conditions of socially and educationally backward classes.

questions.q: After whom was the Mandal Commission named?
questions.explanation: The Commission was named after its chairman, B.P. Mandal.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission's central recommendation concerned what?
questions.explanation: Its key recommendation was reservation for backward classes in central government jobs.

questions.q: In which year were the Mandal Commission's job-reservation recommendations implemented?
questions.explanation: The recommendation of reservations in central government jobs was implemented in 1990.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission's report dealt with reservation as a measure of what?
questions.explanation: The report addressed reservation as a measure of social justice for backward classes.

questions.q: In which decade was the Mandal Commission set up?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission was set up in 1979, within the 1970s.

questions.q: The group whose conditions the Mandal Commission studied is often abbreviated as which term?
questions.explanation: The socially and educationally backward classes are commonly referred to as Other Backward Classes (OBC).

questions.q: The Mandal Commission was a body of what type?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission was a commission set up to study and report on backward classes.

questions.q: Under whose government was the implementation of the Mandal Commission's job-reservation recommendation announced in 1990?
questions.explanation: The V.P. Singh government announced the implementation of the recommendation in 1990.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission is formally known by which name?
questions.explanation: It is formally the Second Backward Classes Commission, headed by B.P. Mandal.

questions.q: What percentage of reservation in central government jobs did the Mandal Commission recommend for the backward classes it studied?
questions.explanation: The Commission recommended 27 percent reservation for Other Backward Classes in central government jobs.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission's recommendations are linked to which constitutional provision empowering the State to make special provisions for backward classes?
questions.explanation: Article 15 empowers the State to make special provisions for socially and educationally backward classes.

questions.q: Reservation in public employment for backward classes relates to which constitutional article on equality of opportunity?
questions.explanation: Article 16 deals with equality of opportunity in public employment and allows reservation for backward classes.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission was the second such commission; which earlier commission of the 1950s had studied backward classes?
questions.explanation: The Kaka Kalelkar Commission of the 1950s was the first Backward Classes Commission.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission's recommendations primarily concerned which category in addition to those already covered by reservation?
questions.explanation: It focused on Other Backward Classes, distinct from the Scheduled Castes and Tribes already covered.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission was constituted under provisions relating to which constitutional article on backward classes commissions?
questions.explanation: Article 340 provides for the appointment of a commission to investigate the conditions of backward classes.

questions.q: The implementation of the Mandal recommendation in 1990 occurred under which coalition government?
questions.explanation: The National Front coalition government headed by V.P. Singh implemented the recommendation in 1990.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission was appointed during the tenure of which government formed after the 1977 election?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission was set up in 1979 during the Janata Party government era.

questions.q: Which Supreme Court judgment of 1992 examined the validity of reservations following the implementation of the Mandal recommendations?
questions.explanation: The Indra Sawhney case (1992) examined the validity of reservations for backward classes.

questions.q: In the Indra Sawhney case, the Supreme Court generally capped total reservations at what proportion of seats, barring exceptional circumstances?
questions.explanation: The Court generally held that reservations should not exceed about 50 percent, save in exceptional cases.

questions.q: In the Indra Sawhney case, the Supreme Court introduced which concept to exclude the more advanced sections among backward classes from reservation benefits?
questions.explanation: The Court introduced the 'creamy layer' concept to exclude the advanced sections among backward classes.

questions.q: Which constitutional amendment, following litigation on backward-class reservations, gave constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes?
questions.explanation: The 102nd Amendment of 2018 granted constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes.

questions.q: The chairman of the Mandal Commission, B.P. Mandal, had earlier briefly held which office?
questions.explanation: B.P. Mandal had earlier served briefly as Chief Minister of Bihar.

questions.q: Following the Indra Sawhney judgment, which body was set up in 1993 to examine inclusion of communities in the central OBC list?
options: The National Commission for Backward Classes
questions.explanation: The National Commission for Backward Classes was established in 1993 following the Indra Sawhney judgment.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission relied on social, educational and economic indicators to identify backwardness; this approach is broadly consistent with the criteria recognised in which case?
questions.explanation: The multi-indicator approach to identifying backwardness was examined and broadly upheld in the Indra Sawhney case.

questions.q: The reservation for Other Backward Classes recommended by the Mandal Commission was later extended to central educational institutions by which amendment-backed measure?
questions.explanation: The 93rd Amendment of 2005 enabled reservation for backward classes in educational institutions.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission report was tabled but its job-reservation recommendation remained unimplemented for roughly how long before 1990?
questions.explanation: The 1980 recommendation was implemented in 1990, after about a decade.

questions.q: The constitutional definition of who may be specified as socially and educationally backward classes was later linked to a presidential list under which article inserted by the 102nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 102nd Amendment inserted Article 342A dealing with the specification of socially and educationally backward classes.
